sections.content:
Now I can see the layout clearly:
- Black F (with white dots 'W'): cols 12-20 (left of center line 'SSS')
- Charcoal F ('c'): cols 42-50 (right of center line)
- The center line is 'SSS' (sky blue)
So the black F is on the LEFT side of the center line, and the charcoal F is on the RIGHT side. They're mirror images of each other across the center line!
Looking at the structure:
- Black F: rows 33-41, cols 12-20 (with white dots)
- Charcoal F: rows 33-41, cols 42-50
The black F is my controlled piece. The charcoal F seems to be a target/template. They're at the same vertical position (rows 33-41).
Now I need to understand the goal. Looking at the images:
- The black F is on the left side
- The charcoal F is on the right side
- The yellow F is also on the right side, lower down
Wait, I think the goal might be to move the black F to match the position of the charcoal F. But they're on opposite sides of the center line. Maybe I need to move the black F to the right side to overlap with the charcoal F?
Or maybe the goal is different. Let me think about what makes sense:
1. The black F is my piece (it moves when I press UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT)
2. The charcoal F and yellow F are stationary targets
3. The goal might be to align the black F with one of the targets
Looking at the current positions:
- Black F: rows 33-41, cols 12-20
- Charcoal F: rows 33-41, cols 42-50
- Yellow F: rows 45-53, cols 51-59
The black F and charcoal F are at the same vertical position. Maybe I need to move the black F to the right to overlap with the charcoal F?
Let me try moving RIGHT to see if the black F moves toward the charcoal F.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Black F at cols 12-20, Charcoal F at cols 42-50
# They're at the same vertical position (rows 33-41)
# Need to move RIGHT to overlap with charcoal F
# Distance: 42 - 20 = 22 cols, at ~3 cols/action = ~7-8 RIGHT actions
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
